nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard de Mortimer was born about 1229, in Tutbury, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. He married Mrs Richard in 1247. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard de Mortimer est né environ 1229, à Tutbury, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Mrs Richard en 1247. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Richard de Mortimer wurde um 1229 in Tutbury, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat 1247 Mrs Richard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Richard de Mortimer è nato il circa 1229 nel Tutbury, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Mrs Richard nel 1247. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Tutbury, Staffordshire, England: イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアトゥトブリー
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Mortimerは約1229年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアトゥトブリーで生まれた。1247年にMrs Richardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Mortimer(은)는 대략 1229년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 튜트버이에서 태어났다. 그는 1247년 Mrs Richard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard de Mortimer nasceu aproximadamente 1229, em Tutbury, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Mrs Richard em 1247. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard de Mortimer родился около 1229 г. в местности тутбури, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Richard в 1247 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Richard de Mortimer nació aproximadamente 1229, en Tutbury, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Mrs Richard en 1247. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
